To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the identity matrix and $\lambda$ is a scalar variable.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{array}\right) $$

To simplify the calculation, let's first find a common denominator for all entries, which is 16. We can rewrite the matrix as:
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
Let $B$ be the integer matrix inside the fraction:
$$ B = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
Then $A = \frac{1}{16}B$.
The characteristic polynomial of $A$ is $p_A(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
Substituting $A = \frac{1}{16}B$:
$$ p_A(\lambda) = \det\left(\frac{1}{16}B - \lambda I\right) = \det\left(\frac{1}{16}(B - 16\lambda I)\right) $$
Using the property $\det(kM) = k^n \det(M)$ for an $n \times n$ matrix, where $n=3$:
$$ p_A(\lambda) = \left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^3 \det(B - 16\lambda I) $$
